title: Count Pairs

content.problemStatement:
Given a sorted array of integers and a value xxx, count the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that i<ji < ji<j and the sum of arr[i]arr[i]arr[i] +++ arr[j]arr[j]arr[j] is less than xxx.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer nnn, the number of elements in the array.
The second line contains nnn integers representing the elements of the sorted array.
The third line contains the integer xxx.


Output Format
Output a single integer, the number of pairs whose sum is less than xxx.
constraints: The array a is sorted in non‑decreasing order
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the size of the sorted array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers in non‑decreasing order. Third line contains the integer X.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of pairs (i, j) with i < j and a[i] + a[j] < X.

solution.approach: Because the array is sorted we can count all valid pairs in linear time using the two‑pointer technique. Keep a left pointer at the start and a right pointer at the end. If a[left] + a[right] < X, then for this left all elements between left+1 and right form valid pairs with left, contributing (right‑left) to the answer; we then move left forward. Otherwise the sum is too large and we move right backward. This scans the array once, giving O(N) per test case. The answer can be up to N·(N‑1)/2, so we store it in a 64‑bit integer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the input array

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static long countPairs(int[] a,long x){long ans=0;int l=0,r=a.length-1;while(l<r){if((long)a[l]+a[r]<x){ans+=r-l;l++;}else{r--;}}return ans;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());int[] a=new int[n];StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());long x=Long.parseLong(br.readLine().trim());sb.append(countPairs(a,x)).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
